text:
HEMATOLOGY/BONE MARROW TRANSPLANT CLINIC
FOLLOW-UP NOTE — DAY +100 POST-ALLOGENEIC HEMATOPOIETIC STEM CELL TRANSPLANTATION

ATTENDING: [Transplant Hematologist]
FELLOW: [Hematology/Oncology Fellow]
PATIENT: Renata Kowalczyk
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
DIAGNOSIS: Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML), FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio (AR 0.82), DNMT3A R882H, normal karyotype, ELN 2022 Adverse risk — status post myeloablative allogeneic HSCT (10/10 MUD, FluBu4 conditioning, PBSC)

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 49-year-old female who presents for her day +100 post-allogeneic transplant assessment. She is doing reasonably well overall and is here for routine surveillance and toxicity evaluation. She reports continued mild fatigue but improved functional status. She notes new-onset dry eyes and dry mouth over the past few weeks, as well as a subtle skin tightening over both forearms.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 49-year-old female with a history of de novo AML (FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN Adverse risk) who was diagnosed approximately 3.5 months ago and has now reached the 100-day post-transplant milestone. For full background: she initially presented with a 3-week history of fatigue, dyspnea on exertion, spontaneous gingival bleeding, easy bruising, and night sweats. Labs at that time demonstrated a white count of 87,400/microliter, hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL, and platelets of 34,000/microliter. Bone marrow biopsy confirmed 74% blasts consistent with AML (FAB M4), with FLT3-ITD at high allelic ratio (VAF 82%), DNMT3A R882H co-mutation, and normal 46,XX karyotype. She was classified as ELN 2022 Adverse risk given the FLT3-ITD at high allelic ratio in the setting of wild-type NPM1.

She received standard 7+3 induction chemotherapy with cytarabine (200 mg/m2/day continuous infusion, days 1–7) and daunorubicin (60 mg/m2 on days 1–3) plus midostaurin (50 mg BID on days 8–21). Her induction course was complicated by febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia (pan-sensitive, treated with ceftriaxone), grade 3 mucositis, and a prolonged neutropenic period of 22 days. Day 28 bone marrow showed complete morphologic remission with MRD negativity by multiparameter flow cytometry and undetectable FLT3-ITD by PCR. Given her Adverse risk classification, she was counseled on and agreed to proceed with allogeneic stem cell transplant in first CR.

HLA typing identified no matched sibling donor (1/2 siblings was a 7/10 mismatch). A 10/10 allele-level matched unrelated donor (MUD) was identified through NMDP — a 38-year-old CMV-negative male. She underwent one cycle of bridging consolidation with intermediate-dose cytarabine (IDAC, 1.5 g/m2 q12h on days 1, 3, 5) to maintain remission during the donor workup period, complicated by grade 2 bilateral cytarabine-related conjunctivitis (managed with prophylactic steroid eye drops per protocol), which fully resolved. Her post-consolidation assessment confirmed ongoing MRD-negative CR.

She was admitted approximately 14 weeks after initial diagnosis for myeloablative conditioning with fludarabine (40 mg/m2/day × 4 days) and busulfan (3.2 mg/kg/day × 4 days, FluBu4) with PK-guided busulfan dosing to achieve target AUC. GVHD prophylaxis consisted of tacrolimus/methotrexate with rabbit ATG (thymoglobulin 2.5 mg/kg/day × 3 days). Peripheral blood stem cells were infused on day 0 (CD34+ cell dose 6.8 × 10^6/kg) without infusion reactions.

Post-transplant course was notable for:
- Grade 3 mucositis (day +7 to +18), exacerbated by methotrexate; managed with IV morphine PCA, TPN for approximately 8 days
- Febrile neutropenia at day +8 (blood cultures negative); treated empirically with IV meropenem
- Mild sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (SOS/VOD) meeting Modified Seattle Criteria — bilirubin peaked at 2.8 mg/dL on day +12, ALT 142 U/L; managed conservatively with fluid restriction and daily weights; fully resolved without defibrotide by day +20
- CMV reactivation: Viremia detected at day +28 (460 IU/mL) on weekly surveillance; pre-emptive valganciclovir initiated
- Neutrophil engraftment achieved on day +14 (ANC >500/microliter × 3 days)
- Platelet engraftment day +22 (platelets >50,000 unsupported)

At her day +30 visit, she had achieved full count recovery (WBC 4,200, ANC 3,100, Hgb 10.2, platelets 112,000). Chimerism at day +28 confirmed 99% donor chimerism by STR analysis. CMV viremia was declining on valganciclovir (460 → 210 IU/mL). Tacrolimus trough was 9.2 ng/mL, within range. No acute GVHD at that time.

Since her day +30 visit, she completed the valganciclovir course with two consecutive negative CMV PCRs (day +70 and +84); valganciclovir was discontinued at day +84. Her counts have continued to improve. She returned to light activities around day +60.

Today, at day +100, she reports:
- Mild fatigue, better than several weeks ago
- Dry, gritty eyes bilaterally — onset approximately 4 weeks ago, progressive, requiring frequent artificial tear use
- Mild dry mouth with difficulty eating dry foods
- Subtle skin tightening sensation over both forearms; noticed some skin discoloration/rash in these areas
- No fever, no chills, no night sweats
- No oral ulcerations or frank pain with eating
- No dyspnea, no cough
- No diarrhea or abdominal pain
- No dysuria
- No new skin lesions elsewhere
- Appetite improving; weight stabilizing

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: Mild fatigue. No fever, chills, or night sweats. No significant weight loss since last visit.
HEENT: Bilateral dry, gritty eyes. Mild xerostomia. No oral ulcers. No epistaxis.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or lower extremity swelling.
Pulmonary: No dyspnea at rest. No cough or wheeze.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. No abdominal pain. Appetite gradually recovering.
Genitourinary: No dysuria, hematuria, or frequency.
Musculoskeletal: No joint pain or swelling. No muscle cramps.
Neurological: Mild residual bilateral hand tingling (attributed to cytarabine-related peripheral neuropathy, stable and improving since consolidation).
Skin: Skin tightening and reticulated rash over bilateral forearms as noted above.
Psychiatric: Mild anxiety around disease recurrence. Sleep improving.
Endocrine/Metabolic: No polydipsia or polyuria. Managing diabetes with metformin, no hypoglycemic episodes.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vitals: Blood pressure 126/78 mmHg, heart rate 74 bpm (regular), temperature 37.0°C (afebrile), respiratory rate 16/min, SpO2 98% on room air. Weight 63.5 kg (baseline 67 kg; mild persistent weight loss from treatment course).

General: Well-appearing female in no acute distress. Alert and oriented × 3. Conversational and cooperative.

HEENT: Conjunctivae mildly injected bilaterally; mild injection consistent with dry eye. No chemosis. Sclera anicteric. Oral mucosa: mild dryness, no frank ulceration, no erythematous plaques; tongue dry. No oral candidiasis. No lymphadenopathy of the cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular chains.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No JVD. No peripheral edema.

Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No adventitious sounds.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly on palpation. Normal bowel sounds. No ascites.

Skin: Fine reticulated erythematous patches bilaterally over the dorsal forearms extending partially to the volar surface, with early lichenoid features — slight induration and papular texture; no bullae, no ulceration, no desquamation. Mild post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation in some areas. No rash on the trunk, face, palms, or soles. Minimal involvement estimated at approximately 7–8% BSA, confined to bilateral forearm distribution.

Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. 1+ bilateral pedal edema (new, mild). Bilateral hand tingling on sensory exam (symmetric, stocking-glove pattern, mild).

Neurological: Cranial nerves II–XII intact. No cerebellar findings. Peripheral sensation mildly diminished in bilateral fingertips; motor strength 5/5 throughout.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

CBC:
- WBC: 5,800/microliter
- ANC: 3,900/microliter
- Hemoglobin: 11.4 g/dL
- MCV: 92 fL
- Platelets: 148,000/microliter

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 139 mEq/L
- Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L
- Creatinine: 0.92 mg/dL (eGFR ~72 mL/min/1.73m2)
- BUN: 18 mg/dL
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
- AST: 24 U/L
- ALT: 22 U/L
- Alkaline phosphatase: 68 U/L
- Albumin: 3.9 g/dL
- Glucose: 108 mg/dL (fasting)

Tacrolimus trough: 7.4 ng/mL (target range 5–10 ng/mL; taper per protocol will begin after day +100)

CMV quantitative PCR: Undetectable (two consecutive negative results at day +70 and day +84 confirmed; valganciclovir already discontinued)

EBV PCR: Undetectable

Schirmer's test (performed in clinic): Right eye 6 mm/5 min, left eye 7 mm/5 min (both below the normal threshold of ≥10 mm/5 min; consistent with lacrimal gland hypofunction)

HbA1c (routine): 7.0% (stable diabetes management)

Chimerism (peripheral blood, day +90 result reviewed today): 100% donor chimerism — complete engraftment confirmed

---

IMAGING RESULTS

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast (performed approximately day +90, reviewed today): No splenomegaly (spleen 10.2 cm, fully normalized). No hepatomegaly. No pathologically enlarged lymph nodes. Lungs clear. No consolidation, nodules, or effusions. No osseous lesions to suggest extramedullary AML. A small uterine fibroid (2.1 cm intramural) is noted incidentally, unchanged from prior. Overall impression: Normal post-transplant imaging, no evidence of extramedullary relapse or opportunistic infection.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 49-year-old woman who has reached day +100 following myeloablative allogeneic HSCT (10/10 MUD, FluBu4, PBSC) for high-risk AML (FLT3-ITD high AR, ELN Adverse). She is in sustained complete morphologic and molecular remission, with near-normalized blood counts, complete donor chimerism, and no evidence of AML recurrence. This is an excellent response. The major new finding today is early chronic GVHD, which I will discuss below.

1. AML — Sustained Complete Remission, MRD Negative
Her most recent bone marrow at day +60 showed MRD negativity by high-sensitivity flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%) with undetectable FLT3-ITD by PCR and 100% donor chimerism on sorted CD34+ progenitors. The peripheral blood chimerism at day +90 confirms complete donor engraftment (100% donor). CBC today shows near-normal counts (ANC 3,900, platelets 148,000, Hgb 11.4 g/dL). CT imaging at day +90 is reassuring with no extramedullary disease. She has no symptoms suggestive of relapse (no B symptoms, no new cytopenias, no bone pain). Overall, her disease status is excellent at the 100-day milestone.
- Continue observation for relapse. Next surveillance bone marrow biopsy planned at day +180 (approximately 6 months post-transplant)
- Continue monthly peripheral blood chimerism monitoring
- Patient and family reminded to report any new fevers, unexpected fatigue, bruising, or bleeding

2. Chronic GVHD — New Diagnosis, Mild (NIH Consensus Criteria)
The patient's new symptoms of bilateral dry eyes, xerostomia, and the newly identified lichenoid skin changes on bilateral forearms are consistent with early chronic GVHD. This is a recognized complication following allogeneic transplantation, particularly with MUD donors, and typically manifests after day +100. The timing and organ involvement are classic.

NIH Consensus Global Severity Staging (2014 criteria):
- Skin: Score 1 (involvement of <18% BSA with lichenoid features; estimated ~7–8% BSA forearms bilaterally; no sclerotic features, bullae, or ulceration)
- Eyes: Score 1 (symptomatic dry eyes with Schirmer's test below normal, not severely impacting daily activities; no corneal ulceration)
- Mouth: Score 1 (xerostomia, mild sensitivity; no frank ulceration or substantial limitation in oral intake)
- No involvement of lungs, liver, GI tract, esophagus, genitalia, or joints/fascia
- Overall NIH Global Severity: Mild chronic GVHD (≥2 organs involved, all mild, no moderate or severe organ scores)

Given NIH mild global severity, systemic corticosteroids are not indicated at this time. Plan for topical/supportive management:

  a) Skin: Topical tacrolimus 0.1% ointment (Protopic) to affected forearm areas twice daily; for more symptomatic areas of induration, add clobetasol 0.05% ointment sparingly under occlusion; counsel on sun avoidance, daily sunscreen use, and moisturizer application; educate patient to report any new skin changes, tightening elsewhere, or bullous/ulcerative changes
  b) Eyes: Preservative-free artificial tears (e.g., Refresh or equivalent) every 1–2 hours as needed; lubricating ophthalmic gel at bedtime; refer to ophthalmology for cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion (Restasis) consideration and further dry eye evaluation; remind patient to wear UV-protective sunglasses outdoors
  c) Oral: Biotene toothpaste and oral rinse for xerostomia; frequent sips of water; avoid alcohol-containing mouthwashes; referral to dental care for caries risk counseling (increased risk in cGVHD with sicca); salivary stimulants (sugar-free gum, malic acid lozenges) may help
  d) Systemic immunosuppression: Continue tacrolimus per current taper schedule — trough now 7.4 ng/mL; given the development of mild cGVHD, we will proceed cautiously with the taper but will not accelerate it. New target trough 5–8 ng/mL. Plan to taper to 5 ng/mL trough over the next 4–6 weeks if no progression
  e) Referrals: Ophthalmology (dry eye/cGVHD evaluation); dermatology (cGVHD skin evaluation and biopsy if needed for confirmation)
  f) Monitoring: Document any new organ involvement, increase in BSA involvement, or worsening of current sites at each visit; institute formal NIH cGVHD scoring at each visit going forward

3. CMV Reactivation — Resolved
CMV viremia (first detected day +28 at 460 IU/mL) was successfully treated with valganciclovir and cleared with two consecutive undetectable PCRs. Valganciclovir was discontinued at day +84. CMV PCR today is undetectable. Continue weekly CMV surveillance per transplant protocol for now; can transition to biweekly surveillance by day +120 given now-stable status and ongoing tacrolimus-based immunosuppression.

4. Peripheral Neuropathy (Cytarabine-Related, Grade 1)
Bilateral hand tingling, attributed to cytarabine from the IDAC consolidation cycle, remains stable and mildly improving. No motor deficits. No new neurologic concerns. Continue to monitor; no intervention needed at this time. Consider neurology referral only if symptoms worsen.

5. Tacrolimus / Immunosuppression Management
Tacrolimus trough today 7.4 ng/mL, within target range. Per protocol, slow taper initiated today with target trough 5–8 ng/mL over the next 4–6 weeks, with a long-term plan for tapering off by month 18 post-transplant if cGVHD remains stable and mild. Given today's identification of mild cGVHD, we will proceed with taper but not accelerate.
- Continue tacrolimus taper per schedule
- Continue TMP-SMX (Bactrim) for PCP prophylaxis while on immunosuppression
- Continue acyclovir for HSV/VZV prophylaxis while on immunosuppression
- No antifungal prophylaxis required at this stage

6. Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
Metformin was resumed at day +90 after confirming stable renal function. HbA1c today 7.0%, reflecting adequate glycemic control in the context of ongoing tacrolimus use (calcineurin inhibitor-related insulin resistance is a known contributor). Encourage continued dietary modification; will communicate with primary care physician for ongoing diabetes management co-management. No changes to current regimen today.

7. Functional Status and Psychosocial Assessment
Patient reports improving energy. ECOG PS 1 (mild fatigue but ambulatory and capable of light activity). Weight 63.5 kg (down ~5% from baseline of 67 kg, stable). She is doing some light household activities and is hopeful about returning to her teaching position. Mild anxiety around the risk of relapse is noted; she is using coping strategies and has strong family support. Referred to our transplant survivorship support group. Social work involvement offered and accepted for ongoing psychosocial support.

PLAN SUMMARY:
- RTC in 4 weeks (day ~+130) for interval CBC, CMPs, tacrolimus level, CMV PCR, and cGVHD reassessment
- Ophthalmology referral placed today
- Dermatology referral placed today
- Prescriptions: Topical tacrolimus 0.1% ointment, clobetasol 0.05% ointment (small quantity), Biotene oral rinse, preservative-free artificial tears
- Continue: Tacrolimus oral (dose adjusted per taper), TMP-SMX, acyclovir, metformin
- Patient educated on signs of acute GVHD progression, CMV recurrence, and AML relapse; instructed to call if fever, bleeding, new rash, or worsening eye/oral symptoms
- Next bone marrow biopsy planned at day +180

Time spent: 55 minutes (>50% counseling and coordination of care)

[Attending Transplant Hematologist signature]
[Fellow signature]
